channel.title: Flying Saucers on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Flying Saucers on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Otis T. Carr and His Flying Saucer Claims
item.description:
Otis T. Carr emerged in the 1950s flying saucer scene, founding OTC Enterprises in 1955 with the stated aim of advancing technology inspired by Nikola Tesla. Carr patented a flying saucer design, the Ezechiel Wheel, and claimed to be developing a full-size version capable of lunar travel. He also invented other devices like “The Gravity Electric Generator” and “The Utron Electric battery.” Despite receiving significant publicity, including from Fate Magazine, Carr’s business ventures were considered fraudulent. He was convicted in 1961 for selling unregistered securities and served part of a jail term.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Otis_T._Carr

item.title: Truman Bethurum
item.description:
Truman Bethurum was a well-known UFO contactee from the 1950s, famous for his 1954 book Aboard a Flying Saucer. He claimed to have been contacted by aliens from a utopian planet called Clarion, located on the other side of the Moon. Bethurum also founded the Sanctuary of Thought, a communal organization. While some believed him to be sincere, many UFO researchers dismissed his claims as implausible.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sanctuary_of_Thought

item.title: Lenticular Reentry Vehicle
item.description:
The Lenticular Reentry Vehicle (LRV) was a secret Cold War-era project developed by North American Aviation under the management of Wright-Patterson Air Force Base. Declassified documents from 1999 describe the craft as a 40-foot, saucer-shaped vessel designed for crewed orbital missions, though there is no official confirmation that it ever flew. While some have linked the project to military flying saucer theories, technical analysis indicates that the LRV functioned more like a conventional space capsule than the anomalous craft frequently reported in UFO sightings. Research into the vehicle grew out of previous initiatives like the Pye Wacket project, which explored the feasibility of lenticular-shaped defensive systems.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lenticular_Reentry_Vehicle

item.title: UFO: The True Story of Flying Saucers
item.description:
UFO (full title: Unidentified Flying Objects: The True Story of Flying Saucers) is a 1956 American semi-documentary film exploring the UFO phenomenon in the United States. The film begins by recounting early publicized UFO sightings and the US Air Force’s initial investigations, including Project Sign. It then follows Albert M. Chop, a public information officer, as his views on UFOs evolve from skepticism to belief in their unknown, possibly extraterrestrial, origin. The documentary analyzes notable UFO footage and culminates with a recreation of the 1952 Washington, D.C. UFO incident, in which Chop played a significant role.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_%281956_film%29

item.title: Tulli Papyrus: Claims and Controversies
item.description:
The Tulli Papyrus is purportedly an Egyptian papyrus from the reign of Thutmose III, with claims originating from Tiffany Thayer in the Fortean Society magazine Doubt. Boris de Rachewiltz claimed to have found a papyrus document describing “flying saucers” and provided a translation. However, Rachewiltz later stated he had not seen the papyrus himself and based his translation on notes taken by Alberto Tulli. Many, including ufologists and those involved in the Condon Report, have considered the papyrus to be a hoax, possibly plagiarized from other texts.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tulli_Papyrus

item.title: The Coming of the Saucers
item.description:
The Coming of the Saucers is a 1952 book by Kenneth Arnold and Raymond Palmer that expands on their earlier articles about unidentified flying objects. The book includes Arnold’s firsthand accounts of his sighting of flying disc-like shapes and his investigations into various UFO incidents. It also features third-person summaries of other UFO reports and is noted for introducing the concept of the “man in black.” The work has been cited as fueling the extraterrestrial hypothesis and influencing later UFO conspiracy theories.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Coming_of_the_Saucers
